title: OCF Research Paper Appendix Schema v1
description: Machine-readable standard for Observable Compute Foundation research paper appendices. Defines the required shape of the Model Reference Appendix present in every OCF publication. Designed for AI-assisted analysis, RAG retrieval, and downstream synthesis.

paper_metadata.description: Bibliographic and provenance metadata for the paper.

schema.description: Schema version identifier. Must be 'ocf_schema_v1' for this version. Do not update when a new schema version is released.

title.description: Full title of the paper as published.

publisher.description: Publisher. Always 'Observable Compute Foundation' for OCF papers.

principal_contributors.description: List of principal contributors in 'Name, Credential' format.

publication_date.description: Publication date. Format: YYYY-MM or YYYY.

geographic_scope.description: Human-readable geographic scope of the paper's findings.

geographic_scope_enum.description: Machine-readable geographic scope. Use this field for filtering across the OCF corpus.

license.description: All OCF publications are open license. Cite with attribution.

series.description: Optional. Name of the paper series this publication belongs to.

ssrn_url.description: Optional. SSRN canonical URL for this paper.

document_summary.description: Single-paragraph summary. Maximum 500 characters. Self-contained — optimized for triage and RAG retrieval. No hedging language.

ocf_named_constructs.description: Definitions of OCF named analytical constructs as applied in this paper. All four registered constructs are required. Additional constructs permitted with a note field explaining scope.

readiness_stack.description: Three-tier OCF model: Tier 1 Foundational Readiness, Tier 2 Digital Readiness, Tier 3 AI Readiness. Each tier is a prerequisite for the next. Provide definition as applied in this paper.

pipeline_collapse.description: Elimination of entry-level positions that historically served as the informal second education system. Provide definition as applied in this paper.

rural_amplification_effect.description: Multiplicative (not merely additive) compounding of readiness barriers in rural contexts. Provide definition as applied in this paper.

access_as_binding_constraint.description: Institutional support infrastructure, not worker motivation or capability, is the primary predictor of workforce readiness outcomes. Provide definition as applied in this paper.

note.description: Explanation of scope for non-registered constructs. Required for any construct not in the four registered constructs.

key_findings.description: Array of finding objects. Every finding must have the same shape. Min 1 item. Use null for fields not applicable rather than omitting the field.

id.description: Finding identifier. Format: F + three-digit zero-padded integer. Sequential per paper.

claim.description: Direct statement of the finding. No hedging language. Confidence carries the uncertainty.

source.description: Abbreviated source key. Must match a key in the paper's Sources Cited table.

confidence.description: Confidence score 0.0–1.0. 0.90+ strong. 0.75–0.89 moderate. Below 0.75 add a note field.

population.description: Population identifier in snake_case. e.g. us_workforce, rural_us, k12_teachers. Null if not population-specific.

timeframe.description: Timeframe string. e.g. '2026', '2024-2026', '2024_present' for ongoing. Null if not time-bounded.

note.description: Optional. Required when confidence < 0.75. Explains the source of uncertainty.

what_frameworks_agree_on.description: Settled conclusions consistent across reviewed literature. Strings only. No contested findings in this array.

causal_chains.description: Named causal pathways as ordered string arrays. Last item in each chain begins with 'Outcome:'. Keys are snake_case chain names.

what_works.description: Optional. Evidence-based interventions. Present in meta-analysis papers.

taxonomies.description: Optional. Categorical taxonomies used in the paper. Each key maps to an array of string values.

series_context.description: Optional. Present when the paper is part of a series. Keys are paper identifiers, values are scope descriptions.

policy_implications.description: Optional. Actionable policy implications derived from findings.

methodology_notes.description: Optional. Methodology metadata for replication and downstream analysis.
